channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Iran launches missiles at Israel for first time since ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Lior Soroka, Sammy Westfall
item.description: The apparent attack Sunday night, Tehran’s first such strike in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

item.title: With Leo and Bad Bunny touring Madrid, the bigger star might be the pope
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: The previously stoic pope is drawing huge crowds and seems to be making a star turn, enthralling the faithful with emotive assurance.

item.title: Cape Verde dreams of World Cup soccer glory, fears U.S. immigration dragnet
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: Migration is woven into the story of the small African nation of Cape Verde and its national soccer team, raising concern about who can attend the tournament.

item.title: An airstrike trapped a journalist. She died as rescuers waited for permission to save her.
item.dc:creator: Suzan Haidamous, Meg Kelly, Scott Nover, Mohamad El Chamaa
item.description: A Post reconstruction of Amal Khalil’s final hours in Lebanon found that Israel’s military denied rescuers access to her during a key period when she was still alive.

item.title: Armenia, caught in Trump-Putin power contest, eyes E.U. and feels Russia’s ire
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: Ahead of a national election on Sunday, Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan has been talking about joining the E.U. and boasting of an endorsement from President Donald Trump.

item.title: Iran launched missiles and drones toward Strait of Hormuz, U.S. military says
item.dc:creator: Maegan Vazquez
item.description: U.S. forces shot down Iranian missiles and drones launched toward the strait and neighboring countries.

item.title: Pope Leo visits a polarized Spain where conservatives are turning on the church
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: On the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years, Leo plans to address political polarization and showcase the Catholic Church as an advocate for migrants and asylum seekers.

item.title: Fourth time could be the charm for Keiko Fujimori, Peru’s most polarizing politician
item.dc:creator: Simeon Tegel
item.description: The daughter of disgraced former president Alberto Fujimori, running again for the presidency, is herself deeply unpopular. So is her opponent.

item.title: Brazil, 24 years from a World Cup title, looks to a foreigner to rediscover its soul
item.dc:creator: Marina Dias
item.description: The Italian Carlo Ancelotti, the most decorated club coach in soccer history, is the first foreigner to lead the most successful nation.
